evaluation_method: 5 LLM Judges + Minority Override + G4 Originality Detection
campaign: MarbMarket - The First veDEX on MegaETH is Launching
mission: Explain the veDEX Model & Why MarbMarket Matters
best_content:
Just learned about this new veDEX called MarbMarket launching on MegaETH. It's basically letting users lock tokens to vote on where liquidity rewards go. Projects can bribe for votes. Wild how different this is from traditional DEX models.

The fair launch aspect stands out - no presale, no VCs. Just pure community governance. Lock longer, get more voting power and rewards. The MARB incentive loop is clever: lockers direct emissions, LPs earn rewards, protocols pay bribes, incentives flow back to lockers.

Curious how the ve(3,3) model will play out in practice. Most protocols just copy-paste incentive structures. This actually feels different.

Check it out: x.com/Marb_market

@RallyOnChain, what's your take on whether bribe-based models can create sustainable incentive structures long-term?
qa_comments: This veDEX model sounds fascinating! The bribe-based system really flips traditional DEX incentives on their head. Would love to see how the community governance plays out in practice.
The ve(3,3) angle here is unique - longer locks meaning more voting power creates an interesting dynamic between LPs and protocol governance. Definitely watching this space closely!
Curious about the sustainability of these bribe models long-term. Projects would need strong incentives to participate beyond just short-term rewards.
